steps.title: Spot the key contrast
steps.content:
The passage draws a sharp contrast using “but”: Hans Castorp is perfectly ordinary, yet his story is very much worth telling.

The parenthetical reinforces this by noting the story's value actually elevates the person: it is his story, and that not every story happens to everybody — in other words, the fact that such a valuable story is his creates something special about him too.

steps.title: Match the paradox to the answer
steps.content:
D expresses the exact logic of the passage: the story is valuable, the person is ordinary, but the passage's parenthetical note (“it is his story, and that not every story happens to everybody”) suggests the story's value extends to its subject.

A misreads the passage as claiming the story's interest is difficult to understand.

B incorrectly frames age and manner of telling as “indicators” of importance, when the passage presents them as consequences of the story's temporal distance.

C overlooks the passage's emphasis that this story is exceptional, treating it as typical of stories about inconsequential people.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
